question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with random consensus timestamp values in the URL path to create unique 'route' label values in requestInflightGauge, causing the gauge's internal label map to grow without bound and exhaust process memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum number of concurrent inflight requests tracked by requestInflightGauge to cause the gauge's internal label map to grow without bound when each concurrent request uses a unique route label?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with random shard/realm/num Hedera entity IDs in the URL path to create unique 'route' label values in requestBytesHistogram, where the combinatorial space of valid Hedera IDs is large enough to exhaust memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prometheus.HistogramVec.With() allocates a new histogram object on first use of a label combination to cause a memory allocation spike: sending N requests with N unique routes allocates N histogram objects, each with len(sizeBuckets)+2 counters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with random network identifiers in the URL path to create unique 'route' label values in requestDurationHistogram, causing label cardinality explosion that exhausts memory and crashes the rosetta node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a circuit breaker on Prometheus label creation to cause a gradual memory leak that is not immediately visible in monitoring, allowing the attacker to slowly exhaust memory over hours or days without triggering rate-limit defenses?
